agent1_snippets.title: Cervical Cancer -- Radiation Oncology -- BT
agent1_snippets.content: BT can be used alone in early-stage cervical cancer or as a boost after EBRT in more advanced disease (stages IB2 to IVA), as it allows for highly conformal dose delivery to the tumor while minimizing exposure to normal tissues. The dose delivery is controlled by adjusting the dwell times within the delivery device. High dose-rate (HDR) BT (>12 Gy/hr) is the most commonly used technique; however, it requires a radioactive source, usually iridium-192.

agent1_snippets.title: Cervical Squamous Cell Carcinoma -- Treatment / Management -- Treatment Of Invasive Cervical Cancer
agent1_snippets.content: Recurrent/ Metastatic Cervical Cancer: About 15 to 61 % of women treated for early-stage cervical cancer develop distant metastases and multiple recurrences. For any suspected case of metastases/recurrence, a PET scan/CT scan is done to identify the metastases. It can be a locoregional recurrence (disease isolated to the pelvis) or a disease involving other organs outside of the pelvis. A vaginal recurrence, if suspected, then the suspected area is biopsied to prove the recurrent disease. The treatment options include hysterectomy or pelvic exenteration for those previously treated with radiotherapy. RT is preferred in those who are non-surgical candidates, provided they haven't already received RT. If the patient is a non-surgical candidate and has previously received RT, chemotherapy is the preferred option.

agent1_snippets.title: High-Grade Squamous Intraepithelial Lesion of the Cervix -- Pathophysiology
agent1_snippets.content: Retinoblastoma is coupled with an E2F transcription factor. Inactivation of Rb uncouples E2F, thus allowing it to upregulate genes required for S-phase entry and progression. The aforementioned E6 and E7 interactions are just glimpses of how these oncoproteins interact with various proteins in the nucleus. Essentially, overexpression of E6/E7 causes cell cycle dysregulation. Studies suggest integration also causes host gene expression changes, amplifying oncogenes and disrupting tumor suppressor genes. As the HPV genome is double-stranded and integrated into the host genome, it effectively evades the immune response leading to persistent infection. Epigenetic modification of various genes and changes in the expression of microRNAs have also been shown to play a role in carcinogenesis. Much remains to be elucidated regarding the molecular pathogenesis of cervical neoplasia. [2] [11]

agent1_snippets.content: Society must consider the significant human and financial costs associated with treating invasive cancer, including its devastating impact on lives and families, compared to the cost-effectiveness of prevention. agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1719
agent2_snippets.content: Infertility in women of cervical origin is classified as a specific type of cervical disorder (اضطراب في عنق الرحم). This condition is a subset of the broader category of female infertility (العقم لدى الإناث). Female infertility is closely related to endometrial implantation abnormalities (انتباذ بطاني رحمي), indicating that disturbances in the uterine lining can contribute to the inability to conceive. Radiotherapy (radioterapie) is recognized as an approved therapeutic option for cervical disorders and for endometrial implantation abnormalities; in certain clinical scenarios where these pathologies underlie female infertility, radiotherapy may be considered as part of the management strategy. Understanding the hierarchical relationship between cervical‑origin infertility, general female infertility, and endometrial implantation, as well as the role of radiotherapy, helps clinicians tailor comprehensive treatment plans for patients experiencing infertility.
